== Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}) | Main hall = {{ill||lt=Irimoya-zukuri|UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill||lt=Daidai Kagura|UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill||lt=irimoya-style|UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill||lt=chidori hafu|UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, compiled in {{ill||lt=Enchō 5|UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}} ({{ill||lt=Meiji 17|UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} and {{ill||lt=Festivals|UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill||lt=Daidai Kagura|UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill||lt=Izumo style|UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

== Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

link|Q11435419}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637|1=River mouth}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458|1=Agano River}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299|1=Echigo Plain}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735|1=Niigata Sand Dunes}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825|1=Double-flowered}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776|1=Kizakura}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710|1=Sai-no-Kami}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oogata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637|1=River mouth}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458|1=Agano River}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299|1=Echigo Plain}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735|1=Niigata Sand Dunes}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825|1=Double-flowered}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776|1=Kizakura}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710|1=Sai-no-Kami}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637|1=River mouth}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458|1=Agano River}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299|1=Echigo Plain}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735|1=Niigata Sand Dunes}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825|1=Double-flowered}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776|1=Kizakura}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kukurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710|1=Sai-no-Kami}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710|1=Sai-no-Kami}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]

Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=January 2026}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Oogata Shrine | Image = [[File:Oogata jinja.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}{{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}} Kawawatari Honmachi | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Sacred object = | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | Established = During the {{ill|lt=Daido|Q295082|WD=Q295082|1=Datong (disambiguation)}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}) | Main hall = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} | Other names = | Pilgrimage = | Annual festivals = Around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}, {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}, first Friday and Saturday of September | Rituals = Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Oogata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oogata Shrine''' (おおがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kawawatari Honmachi, {{ill|lt=Higashi Ward (Niigata City)|Q711707|WD=Q711707|lt=Higashi Ward|1=Higashi-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata City|Q179691|WD=Q179691|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. [[File:Torii at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Torii gate and komainu]] [[File:Haiden at Oogata jinja.jpg|thumb|Haiden]] [[File:Oogata jinja Inside.jpg|thumb|Inside the haiden]] === Overview === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-kami|1=Ōkuninushi}}. The shrine stands in a low-lying, marshy area near the {{ill|lt=river mouth|Q1233637|WD=Q1233637|1=River mouth}} of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|lt=Agano River|Q390458|WD=Q390458|1=Agano River}} on the {{ill|lt=Echigo Plain|Q1045299|WD=Q1045299|1=Echigo Plain}}. Because it sits on a raised section corresponding to the hills of the {{ill|lt=Niigata sand dunes|Q17209735|WD=Q17209735|1=Niigata Sand Dunes}}, it has long been a site where settlements formed. In addition to the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, the {{ill|lt=shrine grounds|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} include a {{ill|lt=kagura hall|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} and several {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrines|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}: Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine, and Furumine Shrine. About 1 km to the northwest, on the shore of the west pond of Junsai Pond, stands Kamimichi Shrine, regarded as an auxiliary shrine outside the grounds. The haiden is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-style|1=UNKNOWN}} structure with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori hafu|1=UNKNOWN}} gable. In the front garden stands a {{ill|lt=double-flowered|Q63825|WD=Q63825|1=Double-flowered}} {{ill|lt=yellow cherry blossom|Q11677776|WD=Q11677776|1=Kizakura}}, making the shrine a noted scenic spot. === History === The founding of the shrine is said to date to the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, during the Daidō era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|WD=Q23819|1=810}}). In the shrine register (Shinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō 5|1=UNKNOWN}} (927), it is listed as Oogata Shrine of Numatare District, one of the five shrines of that district. The “{{ill|lt=Shrine Register|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}” compiled in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji 17|1=UNKNOWN}}) records it as Hakusan Shrine enshrining Kikurihime-no-Mikoto, though when this change occurred is unknown. Plaques and sacred mirrors from about 100 years ago bear the name Oogata Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|WD=Q5268|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19), the shrine name was officially changed to the present Oogata Shrine, the enshrined deity was changed to Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, and it was subsequently ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Festivals and Events === === {{ill|lt=Rituals|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Festivals|1=UNKNOWN}} === * {{ill|lt=Sai-no-Kami|Q115978710|WD=Q115978710|1=Sai-no-Kami}} ritual – around {{ill|lt=January 15|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}} ({{ill|lt=Little New Year|Q137803901|WD=Q137803901|1=koshōgatsu}}) * Spring Festival – {{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}} * Autumn Festival – first Friday and Saturday of September * Oogata Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} ** A {{ill|lt=ritual|Q11588671|WD=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} handed down for about 200 years by the Terayama family, who serve as {{ill|lt=Shinto priests|Q15888735|WD=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}}. During the autumn festival, the doors of the main hall are opened and eleven dances of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumo style|1=UNKNOWN}} are performed and dedicated. Designated as a municipal cultural heritage. === Subsidiary Shrines === === Within the Grounds === * Shinmei Shrine ** Deity: Amaterasu-ōmikami * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto * Furumine Shrine ** Deity: Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto File:Shinmeiguu at Oogata jinja.jpg|Shinmei Shrine File:Inari Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Hurumine Jinjya at Oogata jinja.jpg|Furumine Shrine === Outside the Grounds === * Kamimichi Shrine (かみみちじんじゃ) ** Deity: Ryūrei-shin ** {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} ** Located in Matsuzono 2-chōme, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, on the south side of the west pond of Junsai Pond; established on {{ill|lt=July 30|Q2716|WD=Q2716|1=July 30}}, {{ill|lt=1950|Q12359|WD=Q12359|1=Template: Location map Pakistan}} (Shōwa 25). It has a main hall, haiden, and a stone statue of Kannon Bosatsu. === Oogata Shrine and the District Name === In August {{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 34), the three villages of Shin-Matsushima, Sanga, and Nihonoka in Niigata Prefecture merged and took the name {{ill|lt=Oogata Village (Niigata Prefecture)|Q11435417|WD=Q11435417|lt=Oogata Village|1=Ōgata}} from this shrine. The village was incorporated into Niigata City on June 1, {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|WD=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), and is now part of Higashi Ward, Niigata City; however, the shrine itself is not located within the present Oogata district but in the {{ill|lt=Kawawatari|Q17128357|WD=Q17128357|1=Kawawatari}} district. === Address === *19-25 Kawawatari Honmachi, Higashi Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture === Access === * {{ill|lt=Niigata Kōtsū|Q11502827|WD=Q11502827|1=Niigata Kotsu}} bus '''{{ill|lt=List of Niigata Kōtsū bus routes#E3|Q11502828|WD=Q11502828|lt=E3|1=E3}}''' Kawawatari Line: about a 2-minute walk from “Kawawatari-higashi” bus stop, or about a 3-minute walk from “Kawawatari 2-chōme” bus stop. === References === * [http://www.niigata-u.com/files/ngtphoto/ogtjin01.html niigata-u: Oogata Shrine in Kawawatari, Niigata City, listed in the Engishiki (Niigata’s 100 Scenic Views)] * [http://www.city.niigata.jp/info/bunka/niigatakara/search/detail.asp?eventId=2189 Oogata Shrine | Niigatakara.Net] === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōgata Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:新潟市東区の歴史]] [[Category:新潟市東区の建築物]] [[Category:白山神社]]
